steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses and the transition signal
steps.content: The first independent clause is “Okinaka doesn't make such decisions single-handedly.” The second independent clause is “historical designations must be approved by a group of nine other experts.” The word “however” signals a contrast: though Okinaka sits on the board, group approval is still required.

steps.title: Apply the rule for separating independent clauses
steps.content: Two independent clauses must be separated by a period. When a transition word like “however” appears at the sentence boundary, the correct pattern is: independent clause, transition-word. independent clause. Choice A provides “however. All,” creating the proper separation—the period ends the first sentence while “however” marks the transition to the contrasting statement. Choices B, C, and D each fail: B and C omit the required period between the clauses (creating a comma splice), and D misuses “and.” This matches choice A — A is correct.
